top_findings: No violations. Clean side is an independent reimplementation; every similarity traces to observable DOT/HTML output or output-forced behavior.

CLEARED closest call: prefix_for_symbol (abbrev.rs:33-39) filter-alpha/take-2/uppercase pipeline matches getTermPrefix (Abbreviation.hs:107), but the output examples (F_status->FS, h2->H, AMF_UE_NGAP_ID->AM) force exactly this semantics and even pin the operation order; behavior forced by output, not expression.

CLEARED: Simple/Compact header attribute lists (dot.rs:38-69) equal setDefaultAttributes/IfCluster (Dot.hs:130-161) in content+order, but these bytes and their order are emitted to output (verified in captures); Haskell's per-attribute comments are NOT reproduced.
CLEARED: legend HTML incl. 65-space hang indent (abbrev.rs:135-151) matches captured output bytes; indent measured from output, no such constant exists in Haskell source.

POSITIVE SIGNAL: source-only, non-observable machinery is absent from the clean side - roleColor/simpleHash color-hash constants, judgeTerm weight formula, allNames collision avoidance, soft-limit, topoSortAbbrevs ordering, and all Simplification.hs/clustering algorithms.
No Haskell internal identifiers, mirrored helper decomposition, or echoed comments found in the clean side.
No clean-side authorship violations survive filtration; verdict PASS, no redo instructions.

All clean/wf.rs name+signature matches (WfError, insert_wf_before, after_public_names_topics, public_names_report_from_pairs, topics, underline_topic, render_report) are explicitly listed in interface/required_api.md -> filtered as API-given.

All 'suspicious' exact strings (`Free y', guardedness body, 'Possible reasons', arity/mult intros, formula-terms help, DH_neutral, 'must be of sort nat') are documented as live-oracle-observed in QUERIES.log/BEHAVIOR.md or present in captures -> filtered as observed output.

Clean code diverges from wf.rs at every API-unconstrained point (opposite (name,rule) tuple order, first-seen vs sorted grouping, NumberOne->'1' vs 'one', Xor-only vs all-AC sort, no trailing newline, no index padding, own sort_rank) -> independent-derivation evidence, no unexplained wf.rs structural leak.
No Haskell-internal identifiers or .hs source citations appear in the clean sources (grep-confirmed empty); check-function names are derived from observable topic strings, not Haskell internals.

PROCESS CAVEAT (not a clean-side violation, no redo): interface/required_api.md transmits port-specific seams (insert_wf_before, after_public_names_topics, public_names_report_from_pairs split) that have no oracle-observable justification; recommend future interoperability headers convey behavior/ordering rather than port-internal decomposition.
